item.title: Oil prices soar as U.S. insists Strait of Hormuz open despite ongoing attacks
item.description: The Pentagon insists the Strait of Hormuz is open, but the threat to shipping is increasing, along with oil prices.

item.title: Lung cancer in non-smoking women: "A women's health crisis"
item.description: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

item.title: Melinda French Gates on perimenopause: It took 3 doctors to diagnose me
item.description: Melinda French Gates talked about menopause and her philanthropic focus on women's health in the debut episode of Norah O'Donnell's "Healthful" podcast.

item.title: Melinda French Gates: It Took Three Doctors to Diagnose My Perimenopause
item.description:
Melinda French Gates is focusing her money and attention on improving women's healthcare in America. In Healthful's first episode, French Gates tells Norah O'Donnell why it took three doctors to catch her perimenopause and shares the three simple things that keep her healthier than ever.
We'd love to hear what topics you want covered on Healthful. Text Norah at 202-217-1107

item.title: The Rise of Lung Cancer in Women and Never-Smokers
item.description:
Lung cancer has surpassed breast cancer as the leading cause of cancer deaths in American women—and a growing share of new cases are in women who have never touched a cigarette. Anne Wojcicki's sister, former YouTube CEO Susan Wojcicki, was one of them. In this episode of HEALTHFUL, Norah O'Donnell sits down with Anne, Dr. Kim Sandler, Director of the Vanderbilt Lung Screening Program, and Shira Kupperman Boehler—a lifelong nonsmoker and daily runner diagnosed with lung cancer at 44—to discuss why lung cancer is becoming one of the most urgent, and least understood, threats to women's health.
We'd love to hear what topics you want covered on Healthful. Text Norah at 202-217-1107

item.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane found in ocean 74 years after deadly crash
item.description: The crash in 1952 killed 52 people and led to requirements for the passenger safety briefings given on every commercial flight.

item.title: OpenAI says its technology, on its own, hacked another AI company
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

item.title: Democrats claim Social Security email included "misleading information"
item.description: Lawmakers claim Commissioner Frank Bisignano used an agency email to exaggerate tax savings under the Republicans' OBBBA while inserting partisan political messaging.

item.title: ICE detentions hit highest monthly total in June since crackdown began
item.description: More than 43,000 immigrants were arrested last month as DHS continues to detain people in less-publicized enforcement surges.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha impacting northern Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Agency set to sweep up medical data says some identifying info will be removed
item.description: The Office of Personnel Management said it will "pseudonymize" medical records it collects on more than 8 million people — federal workers and retirees, and their family members.

item.title: Pentagon identifies 4th service member killed as U.S.-Iran war escalates
item.description: The U.S. launched another night of strikes on Iran after four U.S. soldiers were killed in Jordan and Iraq in recent days.

item.title: 3 soldiers killed in Iran strike on base in Jordan were from Texas, Hawaii, N.Y.
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of three U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

item.title: Mamdani says he won't try to arrest Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani conceded late Tuesday that the city does not have the authority to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Mark Lamb wins Arizona House GOP primary after misconduct allegations
item.description: Former sheriff Mark Lamb was running against construction business owner Daniel Keenan in Arizona's 5th Congressional District Republican primary.

item.title: Trump ally Andy Biggs wins GOP nomination for Arizona governor, AP projects
item.description: Trump-endorsed Rep. Andy Biggs will take on Democratic Arizona Gov. Katie Hobbs in November.

item.title: Behind the scenes of Apollo 11 (2009)
item.description: While the three astronauts of Apollo 11 received the bulk of the attention for landing on the moon in 1969, 400,000 people worked behind the scenes to make it happen. Randall Pinkston reported on one of those important people back in 2009.

item.title: House moves to punt government funding fight past midterms
item.description: The continuing resolution would fund the federal government until Dec. 4. It now heads to the Senate, where it could face obstacles.

item.title: What false positive cyclospora test means for outbreak, according to former FDA medical officer
item.description: The FDA is still hunting for the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak after earlier test results proved to be a false positive. Dr. David Acheson, who has more than 40 years of medical and food safety experience, joins CBS News to discuss the issue.

item.title: U.S. launches strikes on Iran for 11th consecutive night
item.description: The U.S. launched an 11th consecutive night of strikes against Iran on Tuesday. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's earlier testimony about the Pentagon's request for additional funding. Then, Washington Post national security reporter Noah Robertson joins with analysis.

item.title: Scientists propose model to protect Earth from solar storms
item.description: If a massive solar storm capable of wiping out power grids across the U.S. was headed toward Earth, could there be a way to stop it? A group of scientists are trying to find an answer to that question, proposing the creation of a satellite constellation called a StormWall to help shield the Earth from the impact of a solar storm. Brian Walsh, one of the co-designers of the StormWall, joined CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Key Arizona primaries to watch today
item.description: Arizona has traditionally been a red state, and six of its nine House representatives are Republicans.

item.title: Voters head to the polls for primary races in Arizona
item.description: Voters went to the polls Tuesday in Arizona for several key primary races. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

item.title: What to know about Pickaxe Mountain, an Iranian target noted by Trump
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday threatened to attack Pickaxe Mountain in Iran, a secure facility allegedly used for nuclear activity. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Australian woman gives birth to rare identical quadruplet daughters
item.description: All four daughters share 100 percent of their DNA after a single fertilized egg split into four embryos, making the pregnancy a one-in-15-million occurrence.

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks officials from deporting witness of deadly ICE shooting
item.description: A federal judge in Texas temporarily barred immigration authorities from deporting one of the witnesses in the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston.

item.title: Democratic lawmakers slam Trump "vanity projects" in shadow hearing
item.description: Lawmakers hammered the president for bulldozing norms in pursuit of his construction goals and imposing his likeness on buildings, coins and passports.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine's top general after days of protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander-in-chief of the armed forces as a government shakeup continues.

item.title: More Americans report shoplifting as inflation climbs, survey finds
item.description: Thirty percent of respondents said they shoplifted this year, with most citing affordability concerns and financial hardship.

item.title: GLP-1s are linked to a 17% drop in worker sick leave, new study finds
item.description: Here's how weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy could reduce worker illness and save companies money.
